meta.note: subscription auth; codex reports tokens, not USD

A.missing: Association is replaced by physical touching, so the act is merely detachment from contact.
A.reason: It describes someone making two touching things cease to touch, not removing something from an association.

B.missing: It lacks an intentional removing act and replaces association with physical nearness.
B.reason: It renders only an event in which two things cease to be near one another.

C.missing: Association is reduced to spatial nearness, yielding physical separation instead of dissociation.
C.reason: Although an agent causes the change, the criterial prior association is represented only as being near.

D.missing: Association is replaced by spatial nearness, so the action described is physical removal.
D.reason: It sharply represents an agent-caused cessation of nearness but not removal from association.

E.reason: It represents an event caused by someone whereby something formerly acting with another no longer does so, adequately rendering removal from association.

F.missing: Association is replaced by mere location among many people, and the resulting exclusion is not explicitly caused by the act.
F.reason: It describes moving something out of a crowd or social location rather than ending its association.

G.missing: Association is replaced by spatial nearness, and the action is not explicitly linked as the cause of its cessation.
G.reason: The before-and-after pattern captures separation but not specifically the act of removing from association.
